summary: Ukrainian forces struck the Russian‑occupied Kerch power plant, causing widespread blackouts in Crimea. Russian troops raised flags in the newly liberated Krasny Liman area of the DPR. Intense UAV and guided‑bomb activity continues across the eastern, northern and southern fronts.
key_points: Ukrainian air defence hit the power plant in occupied Kerch, Crimea, cutting electricity to several districts (Source: @UkraineNow/82129).
Russian forces raised Russian flags in the liberated settlement of Krasny Liman in the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31095).
Russian "Lancet" loitering munitions attacked transport on the Izium‑Oskol road in Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31104).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Kharkiv and Donetsk directions (Source: @kpszsu/66101).
Sevastopol’s air defence shot down nine UAVs, with two more downed later (Source: @tass_agency/382125; @tass_agency/382117).
A railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal was destroyed, disrupting a key Russian logistics corridor (Source: @UkraineNow/82133).
Multiple UAV groups were reported heading toward Olishivka, Goncharivske, Bohodukhiv and Poltava from various northern and eastern directions (Source: @kpszsu/66126; @kpszsu/66128; @kpszsu/66121).
Threats of ballistic missile launches from the Kursk region were noted, including Iskander‑M/S‑300 (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68028).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces struck the Kerch power plant in occupied Crimea, causing blackouts across multiple districts. Russian troops raised flags in the newly liberated Krasny Liman settlement in the DPR, indicating territorial gains. UAV and guided‑bomb attacks persist across the eastern, northern and southern fronts, maintaining high operational tempo.

points: Ukrainian air defence units hit the thermal power plant in occupied Kerch, Crimea, leading to power outages in Evpatoria, Krasnoperekopsk, Saki, Dzhankoi and Krasnogvardeysk districts (Source: @UkraineNow/82129).
A Ukrainian drone carrying five kilograms of explosives fell in Ryuga parish, Estonia, during a June attack on Russia (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31100).
Ukrainian forces reported a night‑time attack on a truck parking lot in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31098).
A Russian fuel truck evaded a Ukrainian drone, indicating ongoing counter‑UAV operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31094).
Ukrainian National Guard commander warned that discovery of an additional 70,000 Russian troops could enable a new Belarus‑origin offensive (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31096).
Russian soldiers raised Russian flags in the liberated settlement of Krasny Liman in the DPR, signalling control of the area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31095).
Russian "Lancet" loitering munitions attacked transport convoys on the Izium‑Oskol road in Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31104).
Russian tactical aviation conducted launches of guided aerial bombs targeting Kharkiv and Donetsk directions (Source: @kpszsu/66101).
Russian forces approached the town of Lyubitske near Zaporizhzhia and engaged in combat there (Source: @tass_agency/382113).
Russian air defence shot down a UAV heading toward Moscow (Source: @tass_agency/382122).
UAV groups were reported heading toward Olishivka and Goncharivske in Chernihiv region from the east (Source: @kpszsu/66126).
UAVs from the north of Kharkiv moved toward Bohodukhiv, indicating pressure on the northern sector of the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/66128).
Guided aerial bomb launches targeted Kharkiv oblast, with multiple reports of UAV activity near Bliznyky, Oleksandrivka, Bzlyuvka and Vasicheve (Source: @kpszsu/66093; @kpszsu/66094).
Enemy tactical aviation activity was noted on the eastern direction, raising the threat of air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/66102).
A Russian "Lancet" attack on the Izium‑Oskol road disrupted logistics in Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31104).
UAVs were observed moving from the north‑east towards Kyiv, with alerts of ballistic threats from that direction (Source: @eRadarrua/97941).
UAVs were reported near the border of Sumy and Kharkiv heading toward Poltava, indicating cross‑regional pressure (Source: @kpszsu/66117).
UAV activity was noted north of Sumy moving toward Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/66105).
MiG‑31K launch was reported as a missile danger across Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/66103).
A threat of Iskander‑M/S‑300 ballistic missiles from Kursk region was highlighted (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68028).
Ukrainian forces struck the Kerch thermal power plant in occupied Crimea, causing blackouts across several districts (Source: @UkraineNow/82129).
A railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal was destroyed, disrupting a key Russian logistics corridor (Source: @UkraineNow/82133).
Sevastopol’s air defence shot down nine UAVs, with two additional UAVs downed later, showing intense aerial activity in the southern theater (Source: @tass_agency/382125; @tass_agency/382117).
Russian attacks on Zaporizhzhia region resulted in 1,050 strikes on 56 settlements, including 18 airstrikes and 769 drones (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
A night‑time attack on a truck parking lot in Zaporizhzhia was reported (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31098).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Kharkiv and Donetsk directions, indicating use of precision strike assets (Source: @kpszsu/66101).

Multiple UAV groups were reported across northern, eastern and southern sectors, targeting locations such as Olishivka, Bohodukhiv, Poltava and Sevastopol (Source: @kpszsu/66126; @kpszsu/66128; @kpszsu/66121; @tass_agency/382125).
Ukrainian air defence downed a UAV heading for Moscow, showing reach of Russian UAV operations (Source: @tass_agency/382122).
Sevastopol’s air defence intercepted nine UAVs in a single engagement (Source: @tass_agency/382125).
Alerts of ballistic missile threats from the east, north‑east and Kursk region were issued, reflecting heightened missile activity (Source: @eRadarrua/97941; @eRadarrua/97923; @mon1tor_ua/68028).

key_developments: Ukrainian strike on the Kerch power plant caused blackouts in Evpatoria, Krasnoperekopsk, Saki, Dzhankoi and Krasnogvardeysk districts (Source: @UkraineNow/82129).
Russian "Lancet" loitering munitions attacked the Izium‑Oskol road, disrupting transport in Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31104).
Russian forces raised flags in Krasny Liman, indicating control of the newly liberated settlement in the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31095).
Sevastopol air defence shot down nine UAVs, demonstrating intense aerial contest in the southern theater (Source: @tass_agency/382125).
A railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal was destroyed, cutting a strategic logistics route for Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/82133).
Guided aerial bomb launches targeted Kharkiv and Donetsk oblasts, showing escalation of precision strike usage (Source: @kpszsu/66101).
Multiple UAV formations reported moving toward Olishivka, Bohodukhiv, Poltava and other points, indicating broad UAV pressure across fronts (Source: @kpszsu/66126; @kpszsu/66128; @kpszsu/66121).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are intensifying strikes on critical Russian‑controlled infrastructure in Crimea, while Russian tactical aviation and loitering munitions continue to pressure supply routes in the eastern and northern sectors. The proliferation of UAVs across multiple fronts suggests a shift toward persistent low‑altitude harassment, complicating air defence allocations. Potential ballistic missile launches from Kursk and north‑east directions raise the risk of high‑impact strikes beyond the immediate frontlines.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command appears to prioritize targeting of energy and logistics nodes in occupied territories, leveraging both conventional strikes and UAV attacks. Russian forces are reinforcing territorial claims by raising flags in liberated areas and employing guided bombs to disrupt Ukrainian movements. Regional air defence units are increasingly engaged, as evidenced by multiple UAV interceptions in Sevastopol and near Kyiv.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source reports indicate a coordinated UAV campaign originating from multiple launch points, targeting both military and civilian infrastructure. The emergence of Russian "Lancet" attacks on key transport corridors underscores a focus on disrupting Ukrainian logistics. Monitoring of ballistic missile alerts from Kursk and north‑east vectors is essential for early warning and civil defence preparedness.
